translations.literal: Traveled inspected commandery springs; opened connected ditches and drains; raised water gates, dikes, and sluices altogether several tens of places, thereby broaden irrigation; year by year increased; many reached thirty thousand qing.
translations.idiomatic: He surveyed springs and streams, dredged channels, and built dozens of sluices, dams, and intake gates until irrigated acreage climbed year after year to thirty thousand qing.

sentences.zh: 民得其利，蓄積有餘。

translations.literal: The people obtained its benefit; stores had surplus.
translations.idiomatic: Households reaped the benefit and filled their granaries.

sentences.zh: 信臣為民作均水約束，刻石立於田畔，以防分爭。

translations.literal: Xinchen for the people made equal-water agreements; carved stone stood by field margins, thereby guard against divided quarrels.
translations.idiomatic: He drafted fair rules for sharing irrigation water and had them cut in stone at the field edge to forestall disputes.

sentences.zh: 禁止嫁娶送終奢靡，務出於儉約。

translations.literal: Prohibited marrying, sending-off, and endings extravagant wasteful; task emerge from frugal restraint.
translations.idiomatic: He banned lavish weddings and funerals and insisted that observances stay plain.

sentences.zh: 府縣吏家子弟好游敖，不以田作為事，輒斥罷之，甚者案其不法，以視好惡。

translations.literal: Prefecture and county clerks' household sons and younger brothers fond of roaming frolic, not taking field work as task—then expelled and dismissed them; severe ones investigated their unlawful acts, thereby display likes and dislikes.
translations.idiomatic: Sons of official families who preferred idle amusement to farming were cashiered; repeat offenders faced prosecution for misconduct as a warning to others.

sentences.zh: 其化大行，郡中莫不耕稼力田，百姓歸之，戶口增倍，盜賊獄訟衰止。

translations.literal: His transformation greatly walked; within the commandery none did not plow crops and force fields; hundred surnames returned to him; household registers doubled; robbers-thieves and prison lawsuits declined and ceased.
translations.idiomatic: His moral sway spread until everyone bent to the plow. Population doubled as families flocked in, while banditry and lawsuits dwindled away.

sentences.zh: 吏民親愛信臣，號之曰召父。

translations.literal: Clerks and people intimate-loved Xinchen; titled him Father Shao.
translations.idiomatic: Officials and commoners cherished him and called him "Father Shao."

sentences.zh: 荊州刺史奏信臣為百姓興利，郡以殷富，賜黃金四十斤。

translations.literal: Jingzhou regional inspector memorialized Xinchen for raising benefit for hundred surnames; commandery thereby abundant rich—granted forty jin yellow gold.
translations.idiomatic: The regional inspector of Jingzhou reported how Shao Xinchen had enriched the people until the commandery thrived; the court rewarded him with forty catties of gold.

sentences.zh: 遷河南太守，治行常為第一，復數增秩賜金。

translations.literal: Promoted Henan grand administrator; governance conduct always was first; again repeatedly increased rank and granted gold.
translations.idiomatic: Promoted to governor of Henan, he repeatedly ranked first in performance reviews and earned further raises and gold.

sentences.zh: 竟寧中，徵為少府，列於九卿，奏請上林諸離遠宮館稀幸御者，勿復繕治共張，又奏省樂府黃門倡優諸戲，及宮館兵弩什器減過泰半。

translations.literal: In Jingning, summoned as Privy Treasurer, ranked among Nine Ministers; memorial requested Shanglin various distant palace lodges rarely visited—do not again repair or furnish; further memorial reduced Imperial Music Bureau, Yellow Gate entertainers, various performances; and palace lodge army crossbows utensils reduced more than half.
translations.idiomatic: During the Jingning era he was summoned as privy treasurer among the nine ministers. He asked that distant lodges in the Shanglin preserve seldom visited by the emperor be left unmaintained, urged cuts to the imperial music bureau, palace entertainers, and court spectacles, and trimmed palace arms and gear by well over half.

sentences.zh: 太官園種冬生蔥韭菜茹，覆以屋廡，晝夜然蘊火，待溫氣乃生。

translations.literal: Privy caterer's gardens planted winter-grown scallions, leeks, and vegetables; covered with roofed corridors; day and night burned smoldering fire; waited for warm qi then grew.
translations.idiomatic: The imperial kitchen gardens forced scallions, leeks, and greens out of season under heated sheds, banking fires night and day until warmth coaxed shoots.

sentences.zh: 信臣以為此皆不時之物，有傷於人，不宜以奉供養，乃它非法食物，悉奏罷，省費歲數千萬。

translations.literal: Xinchen thought these all were untimely things, had harm to people—not fitting thereby to supply nurture; then other unlawful foods—all memorialized abolished; saved expense yearly several tens of millions.
translations.idiomatic: Shao Xinchen held that out-of-season produce harmed health and should not provision the palace; he memorialized to end those practices and other irregular delicacies, saving tens of millions each year.

sentences.zh: 信臣年老以官卒。

translations.literal: Xinchen old aged; by office died.
translations.idiomatic: He died of old age in office.

sentences.zh: 元始四年，詔書祀百辟卿士有益於民者，蜀郡以文翁，九江以召父應詔書。

translations.literal: In the fourth year of Yuanshi, edict document sacrificed hundred regional lords and ministers beneficial to the people; Shu Commandery with Wen Weng, Jiujiang with Father Shao responded to edict document.
translations.idiomatic: An edict in Yuanshi 4 ordered sacrifices to former ministers who had served the people; Shu Commandery nominated Wen Weng and Jiujiang nominated Father Shao.

sentences.zh: 歲時郡二千石率官屬行禮，奉祠信臣塚，而南陽亦為立祠。

translations.literal: At seasonal times commandery two-thousand-bushel led official subordinates performed ritual; offered sacrifice at Xinchen's mound; and Nanyang also for him erected shrine.
translations.idiomatic: Each season the governor led his staff in rites at Shao Xinchen's tomb; Nanyang likewise maintained a shrine in his honor.
